title: Quick Demo - Connecting Local Git with GitHub

content.problemStatement:
Before we dive into GitHub, let’s quickly see how your local project connects to it.

---

**Step 1: Create the GitHub Repository**
- Create a new repo named “demo-repo”.
- Keep “Add README” unchecked.
- After clicking Create Repository, GitHub will show you the **setup commands** you’ll need (as shown below):


---

**Step 2: Create Your Local Git Project** \
Go to your workspace and initialize Git:
```sss
git init
```
**Create a file** named `demo.txt` and add any content like:
`Demo content` 

**Then run:**
```sss
git add .
git commit -m "First commit"
```
**Your local repository is now ready.**

---

**Step 3: Connect Local Repo to GitHub** \
Use the setup commands shown on GitHub :
```sss
git remote add origin https://github.com/username/demo-repo.git
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main
```
---

**After running these commands**, you need to do a **small setup to connect your workspace with GitHub**. \
**Complete the setup first as shown in below video and then try running these commands again**.

  


---
Once done: \
**Your local code will be uploaded to GitHub.**
